model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Beta project card
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (308,151) said dx=148 dy=62 conf=0.90 -> (456,213)
  iter 1: at (456,213) said dx=-74 dy=67 conf=0.80 -> (382,280)
  iter 2: at (419,246) said dx=57 dy=-58 conf=0.90 -> (476,188)
  iter 3: at (433,232) said dx=48 dy=-36 conf=0.90 -> (481,196)
  iter 4: at (445,223) said dx=37 dy=-25 conf=0.90 -> (482,198)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click the "Beta" project card.
The red crosshair is currently at (454, 216) based on the bottom right text.
The "Beta" project card is the second card in the top row.
Its center seems to be around (382, 280) from previous iterations (Wait, let me look at the image).

Looking at the image:
- Beta card is from roughly x=270 to x=495, y=185 to y=375.
- The center of the Beta card is approximately x=382, y=280.
- The red crosshair is at (454, 216).

Let's re-estimate the Beta card's center.
The card boundaries:
Top: ~185
Bottom: ~375
Left: ~270
Right: ~495
Center X: (270 + 495) / 2 = 382.5
Center Y: (185 + 375) / 2 = 280

Current crosshair: (454, 216)
Target center: (382, 280)
dx = 382 - 454 = -72
dy = 280 - 216 = 64

Let's double check the previous iterations.
Iter 4 said dx=37 dy=-25 from (445, 223) -> (482, 198).
Wait, the iteration history says:
iter 4: at (445,223) said dx=37 dy=-25 conf=0.90 -> (482,198)
This means the target was thought to be at (482, 198).
But looking at the image, (482, 198) is inside the Beta card, but not the center.
The red crosshair is at (454, 216).
The Beta card spans from x=270 to x=495.
The center of the card (the whole white box) is more to the left.
Actually, the center of the Beta card content might be different.
The prompt asks to click the "Beta project card". Usually that's the center of the card.
The card itself is a large white rectangle.
Center of Beta card:
x_min ~ 270, x_max ~ 495 => mid_x ~ 382
y_min ~ 185, y_max ~ 375 => mid_y ~ 280

Crosshair is at (454, 216).
dx = 382 - 454 = -72
dy = 280 - 216 = 64

Let's re-examine the crosshair position. The text at bottom right says (454, 216).
The crosshair is indeed at (454, 216).
The Beta card is centered around (382, 280).
So dx = -72, dy = 64.
Confidence: 0.8 seems reasonable.
